mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) signals a strengthening demand for durable goods, which in turn boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of instances, or the signal's rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a weakening supply chain, which in turn compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns in > 70% of instances, or the signal's rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in > 80% of instances, or the signal's rank IC falls below 0.005 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) signals a strengthening labor market, which in turn boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns in > 70% of instances, or the signal's rank IC falls below 0.015 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP (GDPC1) signals a strengthening demand for durable goods, which in turn boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in > 80% of instances, or the signal's rank IC falls below 0.005 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a 1-month decline in housing units sold (HOUST) signal a weakening supply chain and a decline in demand for durable goods, which in turn compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in > 90% of instances, or the signal's rank IC falls below 0.001 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns in > 80% of instances, or the signal's rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) and a 1-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signal a strengthening labor market, which in turn boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns in > 90% of instances, or the signal's rank IC falls below 0.002 over any 1-year rolling window.
